summary: Added an isolated Linux NVENC system boundary around ffmpeg and a safe product-level AV1 encode API. On the reference Linux host, captured one 160x90 frame from a node-scoped Hyprland output and encoded it with low-latency av1_nvenc settings into a tiny IVF AV1 artifact.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Unsafe FFI, if needed, is isolated to an encode-nv-sys boundary.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: No direct FFI was introduced; madobe-encode-nv-sys is the ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary and forbids unsafe code.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Safe encode API exposes only product-level settings.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: madobe-encode exposes DesktopAv1EncodeSettings and CapturedSampleFrame; NVENC flags are mapped internally.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A captured sample is encoded to an artifact.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: captured-sample.png and captured-sample.rgb were encoded to evidence/m2-nvenc-encode-sample/sample-av1.ivf.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Driver, GPU, encoder settings, and command logs are recorded.
Audit focus includes unsafe boundary and artifact validity.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: audit.json reviews unsafe boundary and artifact validity; ffprobe-sample-av1.txt and sample-av1-header.txt validate sample-av1.ivf.

commandsRun.command: nvidia-smi; ffmpeg -version; ffmpeg -encoders | rg av1_nvenc; ffmpeg -h encoder=av1_nvenc
hyprctl output remove/create/configure/remove madobe-qd-m2-nvenc-encode-sample; grim -o madobe-qd-m2-nvenc-encode-sample
ffmpeg raw RGB prepare and av1_nvenc encode with -vf format=nv12 -tune ll
ffprobe, sha256sum, wc -c, xxd artifact validation

nix develop -c cargo fmt --all -- --check; nix develop -c cargo test -p madobe-encode-nv-sys -p madobe-encode; nix develop -c cargo clippy -p madobe-encode-nv-sys -p madobe-encode --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

realWorldValidation.evidence: sample-av1.ivf was produced by av1_nvenc on RTX 4090 driver 595.80.

residualRisks: Direct zero-copy DMA-BUF import into NVENC was not implemented in this node; ffmpeg is the explicit system boundary.
